nextVideo.title: Why I Switched From $800 Waders to These $120 Ones (After 2 Full Seasons)

thumbnailConcept.rightSide: $120 waders with checkmark and happy creator

nextVideo.scriptHook: I spent eight hundred dollars on waders two years ago because everyone on YouTube said they were worth it. Last season I tested a hundred and twenty dollar pair from a brand nobody was talking about, ran them through the same marshes, the same weather, the same conditions. Today I'm telling you honestly — the eight-hundred-dollar pair is currently in my garage. Here's what I found.
hooks: I tested $800 vs $120 waders for two full seasons. One of them is sitting in my garage now.
Everyone told me to buy the expensive waders. I did. Then I tried the $120 option. This is what happened.
You don't need to spend $800 on waders. I tested the alternative for two full seasons to prove it.
reasons: Budget comparison with long-term validation is the top-performing format in waterfowl gear content right now
Early season prep window — waterfowl hunters are actively buying gear in the 8-10 weeks before opener
Affiliate potential is high: wader category has 8-12% commission rates across major outdoor retailers

seasonalAlert.title: Early Teal Season Opens in 8 Weeks — Post Prep Content Now
seasonalAlert.content: Early teal and dove seasons open across most states in 8-9 weeks. This is the peak window for gear prep, setup, and scouting content. Post now to capture search traffic before opener. After opener, search volume drops 60% as hunters shift to in-season focus. The pre-season window is where views are made.

gapAnalysis.title: Budget wader reviews under 200K subs are 2+ years old
gapAnalysis.reason: Current budget wader review content is either outdated or from channels too large to feel relatable. A recent, honest review from a mid-size channel with authentic field footage has clear search opportunity.
